title: Silicon Carbide Converters and MEMS Devices for High-temperature Power Electronics: A Critical Review
authors_or_org: Xiaorui Guo, Qian Xun, Zuxin Li, Shuxin Du (School of Engineering, Huzhou University, China)
claim_supported: SiC devices reach theoretical junction temperatures near 600C vs silicon's 150C ceiling; commercial SiC MOSFET/BJT/JFET devices operate reliably to ~210C with lab prototypes to 727C; four unresolved bottlenecks named: gate-drive design, high-temp current sensing, cross-temperature parameter matching, and packaging maturity.
locator: Micromachines 10(6):406 (2019), full article text
notes: PMC full text fetched directly by scout; verifier independently confirmed title/journal/volume/issue/page/publisher/date via direct Crossref API lookup (works/10.3390/mi10060406) this session -- exact match. Peer review verified per MDPI Micromachines editorial policy. Author affiliation (Huzhou University, China) confirmed via separate WebSearch, not the article page itself.

authors_or_org: Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA)

notes: Consultancy market-size estimate, T3 by SOURCE_STANDARDS definition -- kept in the ledger per explicit instruction to preserve both conflicting T3 downhole-tools market reports rather than reject one for access difficulty (T3 sources are context-only and capped at 15% of the accepted ledger, not held to the same fetch rigor as T1). UNRESOLVED CONFLICT PRESERVED: disagrees with L15-046 (Fortune Business Insights) by roughly 30-40% on 2025/2026 base-year figures ($5.79B/$6.07B here vs. $4.10B/$4.31B there) despite both claiming to cover the same 'downhole tools' market and both citing a similar $7.33B 2030 endpoint -- the underlying market-definition scope is not reconciled and the conflict is shown, not averaged. SOURCE-VERIFIER UPDATE (2026-07-12): L15-046 (Fortune Business Insights) was rejected this session as unfetchable (fortunebusinessinsights.com returned HTTP 403 on every route tried: report landing page, alternate /industry-reports/ path, bare-domain path, and companion press release). Its claim (~$4.10B 2025 / ~$4.31B 2026 / $7.33B by 2030) is no longer in the accepted ledger, but the ~40% disagreement with this record's $5.79B/$6.07B/$7.33B figures for the same nominal market is preserved here as an open flag: even a single accepted T3 estimate for 'downhole tools market size' should be treated as a wide-uncertainty-band placeholder, not a precise figure, given the magnitude of divergence observed between the two consultancy reports that were both live this session. | Quarantined by 2026-07 India-origin rule; may be used only to discover an independent eligible source.

institutions.name: TBRC Business Research Private Limited (trading as 'The Business Research Company', publisher of record on researchandmarkets.com)

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.useful_lead: Global downhole tools market claimed at $5.79B (2025) rising to $6.07B (2026) and $7.33B by 2030 (4.7-4.8% CAGR); independently confirm via a non-Indian-origin market-research firm, oilfield-services trade association, or public-company investor materials before citing any downhole-tools market-size figure.
india_origin_audit.notes: The distribution marketplace (researchandmarkets.com) is Dublin, Ireland-based, but the report's actual producing/authoring firm attributed on the listing page is 'The Business Research Company.' That brand's UK (Canary Wharf) and US (Media, PA) addresses are marketing offices; the controlling legal entity per the site's own copyright notice and CPHI/company-registry records is TBRC Business Research Private Limited, CIN U93000TG2014PTC093061, incorporated 19-Feb-2014, registered office Jubilee Hills, Hyderabad, Telangana, India (ROC Hyderabad). No matching 'The Business Research Company Ltd' entity was found in UK Companies House. This matches the audit directive's flagged pattern of India-registered consultancies using US/UK virtual-office branding; the source is downgraded to discovery_only regardless of the marketplace's own Irish domicile.
